heading: Recomputation of retired pay to reflect advancement on retired list
label: 7362 Recomputation of retired pay to reflect advancement on retired list
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1956ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)399210:594 (last 39 words of 1st proviso).10:1004 (30 words before proviso).(last 39 words of 1st proviso); restated (last 39 words of 1st proviso), ; , .Aug. 21, 1941, ch. 384, § 5June 29, 1948, ch. 708, § 203(c)62 Stat. 1085May 29, 1954, ch. 249, § 19(f)68 Stat. 167(30 words before proviso), .June 29, 1948, ch. 708, § 203(e)62 Stat. 1086The words “basic pay * * * as the case may be” are inserted to conform to the terminology of the Career Compensation Act of 1949, ( et seq.). The words “at the rate prescribed by law for his length of service”, in 10:1004, are omitted as covered by the words “base and longevity pay”. The words “base and longevity pay” are retained to cover the cases of members retired before the enactment of the Career Compensation Act of 1949, and advanced on the retired list after the enactment of that act. The words “and disregard a part of a year that is less than six months” are inserted to conform to footnote 4 of .63 Stat. 80237 U.S.C. 231section 3991 of this title

1982ActThis amends 10:3992 to correct an inadvertent error in the codification of title 10 in 1956 relating to retirement pay of warrant officers advanced on the retired list. For further details, see the explanation for amendment of 10:1405 made by section 1(17).

Amendments2018—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232, § 808(b)(14)section 3992 of this titleSubsec. (a). , substituted “section 7344” for “section 3964”.Pub. L. 115–232, § 809(a)1994— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section contained table with two formulas for recomputing retired pay of enlisted members and warrant officers of Army to reflect advancement on retired list.Pub. L. 103–3371986— revised table generally by striking out provision in column 1 that for a person who first became a member of a uniformed service, as defined in section 1407(a)(2), after , one multiplier is the monthly retired pay base as computed under section 1407(c), substituting in formulas A and B provision that the retired pay base as computed under section 1406(c) or 1407 of this title be multiplied by the retired pay multiplier prescribed in for the number of years credited for provisions that the monthly basic pay or base and longevity pay, as the case may be, subject to footnote 1, of the grade to which the member is advanced on the retired list be multiplied by 2½% of years of service credited, subject to footnote 2, and have subtracted from it the excess over 75% of pay upon which the computation is based, struck out footnote 1, which provided that the computation be at the rate applicable on the date of retirement, and redesignated footnote 2 as 1 and substituted “In determining retired pay multiplier” for “Before applying percentage factor” and “1/12” for “one-twelfth”.Pub. L. 99–348section 1409 of this titleSept. 7, 19801983—, inserted “The amount recomputed, if not a multiple of $1, shall be rounded to the next lower multiple of $1.”Pub. L. 98–94, § 922(a)(8), (2)(G), in footnote 2 of table, substituted “Before applying percentage factor, credit each full month of service that is in addition to the number of full years of service creditable to the member as one-twelfth of a year and disregard any remaining fractional part of a month” for “Before applying percentage factor, credit a part of a year that is six months or more as a whole year, and disregard a part of a year that is less than six months”.Pub. L. 98–94, § 923(a)(1)1982— added formula B, applicable to warrant officers.Pub. L. 97–2951980— in heading for column 1 of table substituted “after ” for “on or after the date of the enactment of the Department of Defense Authorization Act, 1981”.Pub. L. 96–513September 7, 1980in heading for column 1 of table inserted provisions respecting applicability to persons becoming members after the date of the enactment of the Department of Defense Authorization Act, 1981.Pub. L. 96–342

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

Effective Date of 1994 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to computation of retired pay of any enlisted member who retires on or after , to computation of retainer pay of any enlisted member who is transferred to Fleet Reserve or Fleet Marine Corps Reserve on or after , and to recomputation of retired pay of any enlisted member who is advanced on retired list on or after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 103–337section 635(e) of Pub. L. 103–337section 1405 of this titleOct. 5, 1994Oct. 5, 1994Oct. 5, 1994

L. 98–94section 1401 of this titleOct. 1, 1983Amendment by applicable with respect to (1) the computation of retired or retainer pay of any individual who becomes entitled to that pay after , and (2) the recomputation of retired pay under this section, of any individual who after , becomes entitled to recompute retired pay under this section, see , set out as a note under .section 923 of Pub. L. 98–94section 923(g) of Pub. L. 98–94section 1174 of this titleSept. 30, 1983Sept. 30, 1983
Effective Date of 1980 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 96–513section 701(b)(3) of Pub. L. 96–513section 101 of this titleDec. 12, 1980

children.heading: .—Entitlement to Recomputation
children.label: a .—Entitlement to Recomputation
children.text: An enlisted member or warrant officer of the Army who is advanced on the retired list under is entitled to recompute his retired pay in accordance with this section.section 7344 of this title
the member’s retired pay base (as computed under section 1406(c) or 1407 of this title), by
the retired pay multiplier prescribed in for the number of years credited to the member under .section 1409 of this titlesection 1405 of this title

children.heading: .—Rounding to Next Lower Dollar
children.label: c .—Rounding to Next Lower Dollar
children.text: The amount computed under subsection (b), if not a multiple of $1, shall be rounded to the next lower multiple of $1.
